findings.evidence: Grep/read hits: `contractable_of_exchangeable` lines 97-127 and `Exchangeable.blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective` lines 138-174 contain the same `exists_extending_pair`/`map_blockLaw_reindex`/`map_prefixLaw_castLE` proof skeleton.
findings.fix: Make the injective theorem the shared lemma and prove `contractable_of_exchangeable` from it using `hk.injective`, or factor the bounded finite-extension/projection argument into a private helper used by both proofs.
findings.issue: `Exchangeable.blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective` repeats the substantial proof structure of `contractable_of_exchangeable`: zero case, finite bound, `exists_extending_pair`, `map_blockLaw_reindex`, `map_prefixLaw_castLE`, and `congrArg Measure.map`.

summary: One proof-quality issue remains: the new injective-selection proof duplicates the existing exchangeable-to-contractable proof instead of factoring the shared finite-permutation projection argument.
